t_ch: 那些喜欢自动乐团的人，他们根本不是音乐家…他们也许是想要降低演出成本的商人，也许是想随时听到演出的宾客，也许只是想图个新鲜的普通人。
t_en: Those who enjoy the automated orchestra aren't virtuosos... Perhaps they are simply businessmen who want to minimize performance costs, or they could be guests who simply want to enjoy a musical piece, or just ordinary people in search of something refreshing.

t_ch: 演奏的意义、乐手的处境、艺术的未来…这些根本就不是他们在乎的东西，无论我怎么解释，也没办法让这些人共情。
t_en: The meaning of performance, the circumstances of artists and the future of art... These are not things that they are concerned about. No matter how much I try to reason with them, they simply can't empathize.

t_ch: 算了，或许演奏家终有一天会被这些没有灵魂的机巧淘汰吧——就让我在被世人遗弃的边缘演奏至最后一刻吧。
t_en: Forget it. Perhaps one day, all artists will be replaced by these soulless ingenia. Let me perform until the very last moment before the world abandons me and my craft.

t_ch: 但最让我伤心的是…我曾经还以为喜爱谐乐的家族能尊重我们这些演奏家，没想到他们却亲手制造了这些东西…这个给你吧，我已经不想再听他们虚伪的歌曲了。
t_en: What breaks my heart is... the fact that I used to think that the harmony-loving Family respected us musicians. It has never occurred to me that they were the ones behind this... This one's for you, I can't bear to listen to their hypocritical songs anymore.

t_ch: 唉，仔细想想…我所做的一切应该都是徒劳啊……
t_en: Alas, upon reflection... It seems that everything I've done has been in vain...

t_ch: 或许演奏家终有一天会被这些没有灵魂的机巧淘汰吧——就让我在被世人遗弃的边缘演奏至最后一刻吧。
t_en: Perhaps one day, musicians will be replaced by these soulless ingenia. Allow me to perform till I'm forsaken by the world.

t_ch: 布罗娜深呼了一口气，收回了对自动乐团投出的锐利目光——你感到一种知性的气息回到了她的身上。
t_en: Brona lets out a deep sigh, withdrawing her sharp gaze towards the automated orchestra. You feel an air of intellectualism returning to her.

t_ch: 我想起之前跟那个指挥家吵了一架，他告诉我「自动乐团」是有灵魂的。
t_en: I remember getting into an argument with that conductor — he told me that the automated orchestra has souls.

t_ch: 那些东西好像确实和我以前看过的智能乐器不同——它们似乎有自己的思想，有自己的性格，就像是「生命」一样……
t_en: Those things are certainly different from the smart musical instruments that I've encountered before. They appear to be sentient, and have their own personalities, as if they had life in them...

t_ch: 朋友，你说那些小东西真的能明白人类创造音乐的含义吗？
t_en: My friend, you're saying those little things can really understand the essence of music created by humans?

t_ch: 或许能哦。
t_en: Maybe.

t_ch: 自动乐团…也是拥有灵魂的演奏者吗？
t_en: The automated orchestra... do they have souls?

t_ch: 感觉够呛。
t_en: That's deep.

t_ch: 但他们真的会对音乐发表看法，甚至会去了解自己指挥家的情绪……
t_en: But they'll really express their opinions about music, and even seek to understand the emotions of their conductors...

t_ch: 唔…或许成为有机生命并不是理解伟大音乐的先决条件。
t_en: Hmm... Perhaps organic life isn't a prerequisite to understanding great music.

t_ch: 我觉得自己或许该换个视角看待问题了。这些自动乐器说不定能创造新的音乐形式…我可能还要再思考一下。
t_en: I wonder if I should look at this problem from a different perspective. Perhaps this automated orchestra has a new way of creating music... I may have to contemplate further.

t_ch: 能收获对音乐的新感悟，这也是我在宇宙中旅行的意义。谢谢你的启发——这是我曾经为一群悲剧演员献奏时得到的报酬，小小薄礼，不成谢意。
t_en: One of the reasons for my cosmic travels is to gain new insights into music. Thank you for your revelation — this is the compensation I earned when I played my music for a group of tragedian actors. It's not much, just a token of my appreciation.

t_ch: 唔…或许成为有机生命并不是理解伟大音乐的先决条件。
t_en: Hmm... Perhaps organic life isn't a prerequisite to understanding great music.

t_ch: 我觉得自己或许该换个视角看待问题了。这些自动乐器说不定能创造新的音乐形式…我可能还要再思考一下。
t_en: I wonder if I should look at this problem from a different perspective. Perhaps this automated orchestra has a new way of creating music... I may have to contemplate further.

t_ch: 您好。看您的模样，是对店里售卖的豪车感兴趣？都是最新的款式，请随意挑选。
t_en: Hello. It looks like you're interested in one of our cars? These are all the latest models — don't hesitate to take your pick.

t_ch: 现在梦境里的公共交通是越来越发达了，速度也比以前要快，不过…乘坐那些狭窄球笼，终究还是少了些乐趣，您不这样觉得嘛？
t_en: The public transport in the Dreamscape is becoming more advanced, and much faster than before. But... it feels somewhat boring to sit in those Spheroids, don't you think?

t_ch: 既拥挤不堪，高峰时段还经常找不到座位。前些日子的新闻，不知道您有没有听说，有好几位皮皮西宾客，在过路口的时候差点被球笼给撞了。
t_en: They're crowded to the point where you can't even find a seat during peak hours. I don't know if you've heard the recent news, but a few Pepeshi guests were also almost run down by one of these Spheroids while crossing the road.

t_ch: 不算什么大事，但要真遇到还挺闹心的。要是您拥有了自己的车，这些烦恼就全免啦。
t_en: It may sound negligible, but things would've been bad if someone really got run down. If you had your own car, you'd be saved from all these troubles.

t_ch: 你们的车不会出事故吗？
t_en: Your cars won't get into accidents?

t_ch: 我向您保证，这批新车都是经过严格的质检程序才上市的，安全方面绝对有保障，这是我们的售后记录，可以给您翻阅，至今维持着全好评的成绩。
t_en: I can guarantee that this batch of new cars has all undergone stringent quality assurance testing before launch, and they're absolutely safe. This is our after-sales record, feel free to browse through it — they're all highly rated to this day.

t_ch: 你们的车比球笼要贵啊。
t_en: Your cars are even more expensive than Spheroids.

t_ch: 可我们的车比球笼要舒适呀。像您这样尊贵的宾客，出行最讲究的就是*松弛感*，来梦里是为了放松，要是再成天挤来挤去，可就得不偿失了。
t_en: But they're more comfortable than Spheroids. For an esteemed guest like you, *chill and relax* is the name of the game when you're traveling. It'd be a waste if you're being bustled all about when you've come to the dream to relax.

t_ch: 所以您看…这款车是否合您的心意呢？暗金色的车身与您的气质极为搭配，专为速度设计的流线造型也能满足您对时尚的追求，价钱合理，买它不会错的。
t_en: Well... would this car pique your interest? A dark gold car would go really well with your style, and the streamlined model designed for speed would fit right in with your pursuit of fashion. Its price is fair too — you'd be missing out if you didn't buy it.

t_ch: 不喜欢的话，店里还有其他款式。您可以慢慢挑选。
t_en: If you don't like it, there are other models in-store. Take your pick.

t_ch: 带我看看车吧。
t_en: Show me the car.

t_ch: 没问题。这些都是我们推荐的车型，配套齐全，总会有能让您满意的。
t_en: No problem. Our recommended models are fully equipped, so you're sure to find a model that suits your fancy.

t_ch: 可是我不会开车。
t_en: But I can't drive.

t_ch: 呃…您是刚来匹诺康尼不久嘛，梦境里的车都是全自动驾驶，完全不需要进行任何操作，只用激活按钮，输入目的地，接下来交给系统就好。
t_en: Ah... You've just arrived in Penacony. The cars in the dreams are all self-driving. You don't have to drive them — just push the button, enter your destination and let the system take care of the rest.

t_ch: 说真的，您这个问题把我逗笑了。要是需要手动驾驶，我也不会向您保证绝对不会出事故了呀。
t_en: Haha, your question tickles me. Even with manual driving, I can't guarantee that there won't be accidents.

t_ch: 自动驾驶就不会出事吗？
t_en: Does self-driving mean there won't be accidents?

t_ch: 据我所知，本车行售卖的车辆，在梦境里还没有发生过任何事故，刚才的好评记录您也看到了，都是真实评价，不会有假。
t_en: To the best of my knowledge, the cars sold at this dealership have never been involved in any accidents. All the positive reviews you've seen are genuine!

t_ch: 就算…可以给您交个底，真发生了事故，我们也有极为完善的售后服务，绝对不会让您的利益受损，这样可以放心了吧。
t_en: Even if... Well, I can assure you, in the event of an accident, our impressive after-sales care ensures that we will never compromise your interests, so you can rest assured.

t_ch: 你会开车吗？
t_en: Can you drive?

t_ch: …我吗？您说笑了，本车行的豪车都是为您这样尊贵的宾客准备的，我连乘坐的机会都没有，怎么可能还会驾驶呢。
t_en: ...Me? You must be kidding, the luxury cars at this dealership are exclusive to VIPs like yourself. I don't even get to sit in them, let alone drive them.

t_ch: 没什么想知道的了。
t_en: Nothing further.

t_ch: 我对车没有兴趣。
t_en: I'm not interested in cars.

t_ch: 好的，您有需要的时候可以随时过来。
t_en: Certainly, feel free to drop by whenever you require assistance.

t_ch: 是对买车感兴趣吗？我们的车行随时欢迎您。
t_en: Interested in buying a car? You are always welcome at our dealership.

t_ch: 给您推荐的那款新车怎么样，最近的销量挺不错，价钱也合理。您可以再考虑下。
t_en: How's the model that I recommended? The recent sales have been strong, and it comes at a reasonable price. It might be worth your consideration.

t_ch: 怎么说，挣钱好像也没想得那么难嘛！
t_en: How do I put this, guess it's not that hard to earn money!

t_ch: …心情好得就像是连着喝了十几杯甜味饮料，连身边的风都冒着气泡！
t_en: ...I feel as if I've drunk a dozen sweet drinks in a row, even the wind is bubbling around me!

t_ch: 昨天是销售冠军，今天的成交量眼看着也要登顶，真是要感谢每位愿意光顾的客人——就是您吧！您就是我的苏乐达，我的快乐之源！
t_en: Yesterday we were top in sales, and it looks like we're going to repeat that feat again today. It's all thanks to the customers who are willing to support us — that must be you! You really are my SoulGlad, my source of happiness!

t_ch: 不再多逛逛嘛，我觉着呀，您和这些豪车就是绝配！都散发着苜蓿币的香味！
t_en: Don't you want to take a closer look? Personally, I feel our luxury cars and you are a match made in heaven! It radiates with the fragrance of Alfalfa credits!

t_ch: 你感到一股由衷的喜悦从身旁传来，仿佛寒冷的夜已经过去，最灿烂的梦即将实现，你不由得翘首以盼，沉陷在这份对明天的纯粹希望中。
t_en: You feel a wave of joy emanating from your side, as if the chilling night has passed, revealing a most splendid dream about to come true. You can't help but look forward to it, sinking into a pure hope for tomorrow.

t_ch: …心情好得就像是连着喝了十几杯甜味饮料，连身边的风都冒着气泡！
t_en: ...I feel as if I've drunk a dozen sweet drinks in a row, even the wind is bubbling around me!

t_ch: 昨天是销售冠军，今天的成交量眼看着也要登顶，真是要感谢每位愿意光顾的客人——就是您吧！您就是我的苏乐达，我的快乐之源！
t_en: Yesterday we were top in sales, and it looks like we're going to repeat that feat again today. It's all thanks to the customers who are willing to support us — that must be you! You really are my SoulGlad, my source of happiness!

t_ch: 不再多逛逛嘛，我觉着呀，您和这些豪车就是绝配！都散发着苜蓿币的香味！
t_en: Don't you want to take a closer look? Personally, I feel our luxury cars and you are a match made in heaven! It radiates with the fragrance of Alfalfa credits!

t_ch: 这个月到手的工资变少，都是那些人的错！
t_en: There's a cut in this month's paycheck, and it's all because of those people!

t_ch: 啧，那群猎犬都是贪婪…永远不会满足的人，该叫它们白眼狼！
t_en: Pft, those greedy hounds... They are never satisfied. More like freeloading dogs!

t_ch: 前几天，就在主干道上发生的那起车祸…明明以前花点钱就能摆平的，这次非得说，因为开车的是位皮皮西富商，要多送点礼物才能让他消气。
t_en: A few days ago, an accident happened on one of the main roads... It could've been resolved with some compensation, but he insisted that more gifts were required since it was a rich Pepeshi businessman driving the car.

t_ch: 就是他们自己私吞了！还要威胁我们，说不给钱就要把伪造好评的事传出去！奸诈！
t_en: They're the ones who took the money! They have the audacity to threaten us, saying that if we don't pay up, they'll leak the matter of us faking our reviews! So scummy!

t_ch: 为了抹掉事故记录，这个月的公关成本上升了不少…哎，奖金又要变少了。
t_en: In order to erase the records of the accident, this month's public relations costs have gone up... *sigh* My bonus is going to shrink again.

t_ch: 还有！我非得要喷了，好好的自动驾驶系统不用，坚持要用手操控，这不出事才怪嘛！有时候真不知道那些皮皮西人的脑子里都在想什么！
t_en: Also! I really have to say something about this: They refuse to use the self-driving function and insist on driving manually, of course there will be an accident! I really don't know what these Pepeshi are thinking!

t_ch: 哎，最开始就不该相信他们的……
t_en: *sigh* Shouldn't have trusted them in the first place...

t_ch: 我真傻…真不该劝老爸来这买车的。
t_en: I'm so stupid... I shouldn't have convinced my dad to buy a car here.

t_ch: …手头的员工优惠始终没用，正好他原本的车也该换了，就想着…趁这个机会，占点车行的便宜。内部价，再打上折扣还真挺实惠的。
t_en: ...I never used my employee's discount, and it was time to replace his car. So, I saw it as a good opportunity to take advantage of the dealership — the discounted internal price is actually quite a steal!

t_ch: 结果…给我们的是仓库里积压了好几年的老型号。不仅驾驶系统早就过时了，需要全部重新安装，就连表面的喷漆都开始变色，尽是怪味……
t_en: In the end... we were given an outdated model that had been sitting in storage for years. Not only was the driving system obsolete and needed to be replaced, the paint was discolored, and it also had a strange odor...

t_ch: 这哪是员工福利呀，分明就是把卖不出去的产品低价清仓…还吃准了我不敢投诉的想法，品牌形象受影响，我的奖金也要减少。哎。
t_en: How is this to the employees' benefit? It's clearly just clearance for unsold stock... They knew I wouldn't dare complain about fear of affecting the brand's image and my bonus taking a hit... Argh!

t_ch: 能怎么办呢，就当是个教训，想保住工作，就必须吃这个亏…我真傻。
t_en: What can I do about it? I'll just take it as a lesson learned. If I want to keep this job, I'll just have to take this... I'm so stupid!

t_ch: 欢迎光临！店里都是刚到的衣服，时尚潮流，款式齐全，您可以随意挑选。
t_en: Welcome! The store has a new season of arrivals, fashionable and stylish with an array of designs, take your pick!

t_ch: 要是有喜欢的，我可以帮您看看折扣，价钱绝对公道。
t_en: If you see something you like, I can help you check if there's a discount, it'll be a steal!

t_ch: 有什么推荐的吗？
t_en: Do you have any recommendations?

t_ch: 这些是近期颇受好评的单品，挑出来供您选购。
t_en: These are some of the bestsellers recently, I've picked them out for you to choose from!

t_ch: 你在这工作多久了？
t_en: How long have you worked here?

t_ch: …您是问我吗？没多长时间，原先的那位店员辞职了，我才刚从其他店调过来。
t_en: ...Are you asking me? I've only been here for a while, the previous staff quit and I just got transferred here from another store.

t_ch: 请放心，我从事服务行业也好几年了，会竭力保障您愉快的购物体验。
t_en: Please be assured, I have been in the service industry for several years. I will do my utmost to ensure that you have a delightful shopping experience.

t_ch: …我就是到处逛逛。
t_en: ...I'm just window shopping.

t_ch: 不打扰您了，有需要随时喊我就行。
t_en: I won't impose any further, please feel free to reach out to me if you require any assistance.

t_ch: 为您服务是我的荣幸。
t_en: It's an honor to serve you.

t_ch: 对店里的商品还满意吗？有任何意见，都可以直接向我提出，我们会尽全力改进。
t_en: Are you satisfied with the items in our store? If you have any suggestions, feel free to let me know and we will do our best to improve.

t_ch: 我得好好干，绝不能浪费这个来之不易的机会！
t_en: I'll have to do my best, I can't afford to let this opportunity go to waste!

t_ch: …总算在梦境里找到了份稳定体面的工作，待遇还不错！我得好好干，绝不能浪费这个来之不易的机会。
t_en: ...I finally found a decent job in the Dreamscape with decent benefits! I'll have to do my best, I can't afford to let this opportunity go to waste!

t_ch: 感谢家族的提拔，感谢我妻子的支持和照顾……
t_en: It's all thanks to The Family for the promotion, and my wife's for her care and support...

t_ch: …能怎么办？继续活着吧。
t_en: ...What can we do? We can only keep on living.

t_ch: …有时候真觉得很累啊。
t_en: ...It does feel exhausting sometimes.

t_ch: 店里的营收压力很大，家族那边也逼得紧，孩子的学费没着落，生活开销倒水涨船高。
t_en: The pressure for in-store sales is huge, and The Family's demands are pressing. My child's tuition is not settled and the cost of living is going through the roof.

t_ch: 不知道当时，柯柯娜是怎么撑下来的…她既然选择离职，肯定找到了更好的去处吧。
t_en: I have no idea how Cocona even endured this ordeal... If she chose to resign, that must mean she found something better.

t_ch: 真想看看那些规矩是哪个大聪明想出来的！
t_en: I'd really like to see who's the genius who came up with these rules!

t_ch: …都是些什么破规定！
t_en: ...What kind of crap rules are these!

t_ch: 「时刻谨记：客人的话都是对的，客人的指责都有道理，客人的意见必须听从，客人的期待就是你的目标…如果收到差评，无条件扣除半个月工资，并取消晋升资格。」
t_en: "Always bear in mind: The customer's words are always right, the customer's complaints are always practical, the customer's opinions must be adhered to, the customer's expectation is your goal... If poor reviews are received, half a month's pay will be docked without question, and promotion evaluation will not be considered."

t_ch: 管得这么严，还让不让人活了！
t_en: So strict! How about we just dig ourselves a grave!?

t_ch: 啊，没想到连着喝下<unbreak>40</unbreak>瓶苏乐达会是这种滋味。真是…太爽啦！
t_en: Ah, who would've thought downing forty bottles of SoulGlad back-to-back would feel like this. It feels... absolutely amazing!

t_ch: 我一定要把这感觉记下来…那边那个，你也要加入派对？
t_en: I have to note this feeling down... You, over there, do you want to join the party?

t_ch: 你看上去不太好。
t_en: You don't look so good...

t_ch: 别担心，这只是一点小小的…兴奋过度。梦里的苏乐达，喝不坏身子的！
t_en: Don't worry about me. I'm just a little... over-excited. Inside a dream, drinking SoulGlad doesn't harm your body!

t_ch: 何况我可是丹西，「黄金的时刻」最逍遥的派对客，怎么会因为玩乐倒下？
t_en: Plus, I'm Dancy! I am Golden Hour's most legendary partygoer. You think a bit of fun is enough to take me out?

t_ch: 什么派对？
t_en: What party?

t_ch: 还能是什么？「黄金的时刻」无时无刻不在举办派对，需要自备的只有一颗爱玩的心。
t_en: What party do you think? Golden Hour is one non-stop party! All you need to bring is a fun-loving spirit!

t_ch: 而我，就是这里最逍遥的派对客，丹西！
t_en: As for me, I'm the most legendary partygoer here, Dancy!

t_ch: 看我的皮皮西后援团就知道，我可比一般人会玩多了。整个匹诺康尼就没有我不敢尝试的娱乐！
t_en: You can tell by my Pepeshi entourage that I am the biggest partygoer around! There is no form of entertainment in Penacony that I wouldn't dare try!

t_ch: 要是你也愿意成为我的粉丝——更准确地说，派对搭子——我就可以和你分享我源源不断的<color=#f29e38ff>「艾迪恩代币」</color>，让你在梦里尽情畅玩。
t_en: If you were willing to become my fan — Or, my party partner, to be more precise — I can share my endless <color=#f29e38ff>"Aideen Tokens"</color> with you, so you can play to your heart's content inside the dream.

t_ch: 怎么样，够意思吧？
t_en: Well, pretty tempting, right?

t_ch: 好啊，我同意。
t_en: Alright, I'll do it!

t_ch: 好！从现在起，我们就是共同进退的玩伴了。
t_en: Okay! From now on, we are ride-or-die party partners!

t_ch: 为了游戏币…
t_en: Just for some tokens...?

t_ch: 别那么没精神嘛~从现在起，我们就是共同进退的玩伴了！
t_en: Don't say it like that! From now on, we are ride-or-die party partners!

t_ch: 但是，我拒绝。
t_en: Thanks, I hate it.

t_ch: 这…没关系，照样给！谁让咱们都是来匹诺康尼玩的逐梦客呢，哈哈~
t_en: That... It's fine. I'll give them to you anyway! After all, we are all Dreamchasers here in Penacony, haha~

t_ch: 来，干了这瓶苏乐达！让我们一起拥抱「糖浆主义」！
t_en: Come, drink this SoulGlad! Let us embrace syrupism together!

t_ch: 今天的朋友费结一下？
t_en: So, what about today's friendship fee?

t_ch: 别急嘛，我正要给你呢。
t_en: What's the hurry! I was just about to give it to you!

t_ch: 我想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to chat with you.

t_ch: 玩得开心，朋友，别让梦境留有遗憾！
t_en: Have fun, my friend! Don't leave any regrets in the Dreamscape!

t_ch: 如何评价匹诺康尼的美梦？
t_en: How would you rate the dreams of Penacony?

t_ch: 绝赞！我就这么说吧，如果不是生存所迫，谁不想一辈子待在梦里？！
t_en: Super awesome! Let me put it this way, if it weren't a matter of survival, I don't think anyone would ever want to leave this dream!

t_ch: 你要知道，对绝大多数普通人来说，现实只会带来一肚子负面情绪，唯有在梦中才能尽情释放自我。
t_en: What you have to understand is that for most regular people, reality only brings a world of negative emotions. It's only in the dream world that we can truly unleash our inner selves!

t_ch: 再没有哪个地方比梦境更适合生活了。这里就是天堂，是抚慰人心的魔法泉。
t_en: There is no better place to live. This is paradise, and a magical spring that soothes our souls.

t_ch: 创造这一切的「钟表匠」简直是大众灵魂的救星！
t_en: "The Watchmaker" that created this place, is truly the people's savior!

t_ch: 而我，派对客丹西，将用玩乐来向他献上最崇高的敬意！
t_en: As for me, Dancy the partygoer, I will use fun as my way of expressing my greatest respect to him!

t_ch: 如何评价「黄金的时刻」？
t_en: How do you rate Golden Hour?

t_ch: 我最喜欢的「时刻」，没有之一。十分满分的话我给它九点九分。
t_en: It is my favorite Moment, without exception. I'd give it <unbreak>9.9</unbreak> out of <unbreak>10</unbreak>!

t_ch: 谁没有过在夜幕下眺望高楼的经历呢？每每看着这座永不熄灯的城市，我就感到…安心。
t_en: Who hasn't stared up at the skyscrapers under the cover of night? Whenever I see this city that never sleeps, I feel... at peace.

t_ch: 即使什么娱乐设施都没有，我也愿意待在栏杆边上，对着远处的楼宇发呆一整天。
t_en: Even if there were no fun and games on offer, I'd be happy to stand by the rail and stare at the distant buildings for a whole day.

t_ch: 我相信它肯定抓住了我们心底的什么东西。
t_en: I am convinced that the city has captured something in all our hearts.

t_ch: 有什么好玩的推荐吗？
t_en: Do you have any fun recommendations?

t_ch: 「黄金的时刻」你肯定已经玩了个八九不离十，我就不再赘述了。
t_en: I know you must have played almost everything Golden Hour has to offer by now, so I won't keep babbling on...

t_ch: 之前听新闻说，家族正在扩建一处全新的梦境空间。那地方好像叫什么「筑梦边境」，暂时还不让外人参观。
t_en: I heard on the news that The Family is building a brand-new Dreamscape. They're calling it "Dream's Edge" or something like that, but outsiders aren't allowed in for now.

t_ch: 不过，能被家族叫来当筑梦师的家伙肯定都很会玩。你要是有办法溜去那里，指不定能偷学两招。
t_en: Although, I just know that anyone that The Family would call to be a Dreamweaver must be super fun. If you could find a way to sneak in, maybe you could learn a few tricks from them.

t_ch: 唉~再不快点出新地图的话，逐梦客们迟早也会厌倦这里吧……
t_en: Ugh, if they don't drop a new map soon, Dreamchasers will get bored with this place...

t_ch: 每周都有艾迪恩代币拿吗？
t_en: Are there Aideen Tokens to grab every week?

t_ch: 当然…不过每周只能拿一次。
t_en: Of course... But only once a week.

t_ch: 我需要一些时间把它们…取出来。对，从我的小金库里取出来。
t_en: I need some time to... withdraw them. That's right. To withdraw them from my personal vault.

t_ch: 在此期间，你先随便玩点其他的什么，之后再来找我谈这个话题吧。
t_en: In the meantime, go and play something else for a while. We can discuss this topic again later.

t_ch: 就这样吧，拜拜。
t_en: That's all. Bye-bye.

t_ch: 玩得开心，朋友，别让梦境留有遗憾！
t_en: Have fun, my friend! Don't leave any regrets in the Dreamscape!

t_ch: 是你啊，我的朋友。让我们…嗨起来？
t_en: It's you, my friend! Shall we... party?

t_ch: 丹西的表情开始凝固，就像冷却下来的热油。
t_en: Dancy's expression began to freeze, like hot wax solidifying as it cools.

t_ch: 奇怪，我感觉…有点累。难道是最近嗨过头了？
t_en: That's strange. I feel... a little tired. Maybe I have overdone the partying a little lately?

t_ch: 不对，现在不是思考这些的时候。如果不赶紧玩的话，我来「黄金的时刻」不就失去意义了吗？
t_en: No, now is not the time to be thinking such things! If I don't start having fun right now, then coming to Golden Hour loses all meaning. Don't you think?

t_ch: 可惜，没有了激情，再热闹的派对也嗨不起来了。
t_en: Unfortunately, a party without passion will never be a true blast.

t_ch: 为什么人只有在梦中才能放松自己呢……
t_en: Why is it that I can only ever relax inside the dream world...

t_ch: 唉，为什么人只有在梦中才能放松自己呢……
t_en: Ah, why is it that people can only ever relax once they are in the dream world...

t_ch: 丹西的表情低落下来，欲哭无泪。
t_en: Dancy's expression suddenly dropped, as if they were about to cry.

t_ch: 人们总是往我身上贴标签，什么酒店经理，什么完美精英…就好像我不用上厕所一样。
t_en: People are always trying to put labels on me... such as "hotel manager," "perfect elite"... It's like I'm not even a human that uses the toilet just like everyone else.

t_ch: 有时我也会感到劳累，也想摆脱这些印象…可大家的期许已经成了我的面具，戴上容易，摘下来难……
t_en: I feel tired too, sometimes. I want to escape these impressions... But people's expectations have become a mask for me; easy to wear, but very hard to remove...

t_ch: 到头来，我只有在梦里、变成别人认不出的样子，才能稍稍展露本心……
t_en: In the end, it's only in the dream world where I can become someone that no one recognizes that I can truly show my authentic self.

t_ch: 哈，我可比看上去不正经多了！
t_en: Hah, I'm a lot less serious than I look!

t_ch: 丹西的神态突然开始扭捏起来，眉头时而紧锁、时而舒展……
t_en: Dancy's expression began to warp, brow furrowing and relaxing...

t_ch: 现实酒店里的那些年轻服务员，每次一见我就怕得要死，却在背地里联合起来说我苛刻。
t_en: Those waiters in the hotel in reality, every time they see me, they're scared half to death. Then, when they're behind my back, they gang up and say that I'm cruel.

t_ch: 估计他们还以为我很不食人间烟火。哈，其实都是假正经！
t_en: They probably think that I am stuck-up and consider myself superior to everyone around me. Hah! My seriousness is all an act!

t_ch: 真以为我喜欢总是板着个脸啊？还不是因为有人敬酒不吃吃罚酒！
t_en: You think I enjoy always having a stern face on? I have to be like this, or they'll walk all over me!

t_ch: 也亏是能吓住他们……
t_en: At least my seriousness scared them into behaving...

t_ch: 朋友，人不疯玩枉少年！
t_en: My friend! No Party, No Life!

t_ch: 仿佛播碟机切到了一张劲爆的摇滚专辑，丹西的脸突然换上了一副激动的表情。
t_en: Dancy's expression suddenly became super excited, as if a jukebox had suddenly started blasting an electrifying rock anthem.

t_ch: 呜呼，真是人不疯玩枉少年啊！
t_en: Woohoo! No Party, No Life! My friend!

t_ch: 何必反思过去？何必焦虑未来？我们唯一要做的就是享受当下，纵情玩耍。
t_en: Why dwell on the past? Why worry about the future? The only thing we need to do is enjoy the moment and have fun!

t_ch: 这就是苏萨先生的《糖浆主义宣言》，你们都该明白这个道理！
t_en: That is precisely Mr. Sousa's Syrupism Manifesto. Make sure you understand this lesson!

t_ch: 随着他的动作幅度逐渐加大，各种东西从他口袋里接二连三地掉出……
t_en: As his movements grew larger and more exaggerated, various things began to fall out of his pockets, one after the other...

t_ch: 仿佛播碟机切到了一张劲爆的专辑，丹西的脸突然换上了一副激动的表情。
t_en: Dancy's expression suddenly became super excited, as if a CD player had suddenly started blasting a power ballad.

t_ch: 呜呼，真是人不疯玩枉少年啊！
t_en: Woohoo! No Party, No Life! My friend!

t_ch: 何必反思过去？何必焦虑未来？我们唯一要做的就是享受当下，纵情玩耍。
t_en: Why dwell on the past? Why worry about the future? The only thing we need to do is enjoy the moment and have fun!

t_ch: 这就是苏萨先生的《糖浆主义宣言》，你们都该明白这个道理！
t_en: That is precisely Mr. Sousa's Syrupism Manifesto. Make sure you understand this lesson!

t_ch: 这位朋友，请留步，容我采访你一下——请问你知道什么是「二次元」吗？
t_en: My friend, please allow me a moment. I would like to interview you — Are you familiar with "anime"?

t_ch: 我不懂二次元，你教教我吧。
t_en: I'm not. Tell me about it.

t_ch: 唉…我以前以为我懂什么是二次元，但事到如今，我也搞不清楚了…到底什么是二次元呢？
t_en: Oh... I used to think I understood what anime was but recently, I can't seem to tell... Just exactly what IS anime?

t_ch: 让我来教教你，我是动漫高手。
t_en: Let me teach you. I am an anime expert.

t_ch: 你？动漫高手？不是我看不起你啊，要论「动漫高手」，在这匹诺康尼还没人比我配得上这称呼…可现在连我，都搞不懂什么是二次元了……
t_en: You? An anime expert? It's not that I am underestimating your knowledge. It's just that no one in Penacony is more fitting for the title of anime expert than I... It's just that now, even I cannot understand what anime is anymore...

t_ch: 你自己不就是二次元角色？
t_en: Are you not yourself an anime character?

t_ch: 你真有意思，我怎么会是二次元角色呢？人家二次元角色要么酷炫要么可爱，我配吗？
t_en: What a strange thing to say. How could I be an anime character? Anime characters are all either cool or cute. You think I live up to that?

t_ch: 我原本来到这匹诺康尼，是为了拓展自己作为二次元艺术家的眼界，可事到如今，倒是陷入了困惑，不知道到底什么才算是二次元了……
t_en: I originally came to Penacony to expand my outlook as an anime artist but... I find that I have fallen into confusion and no longer know what anime even IS anymore...

t_ch: 唉…我一直以为自己多多少少也算是当世泰斗了，可现在却觉得，自己远比自己想象中要浅薄。
t_en: Ah... I always thought that I was an authority in the modern anime world... But now I feel like I am far less knowledgeable than I imagined.

t_ch: 我们聊聊二次元吧。
t_en: Let's talk about anime.

t_ch: 好啊。也许和你聊聊，也能帮我拓宽一些思路，走出泥沼。
t_en: Alright. Perhaps through talking with you I will be able to expand my thinking and escape this slump.

t_ch: 你为啥觉得自己搞不懂二次元？
t_en: Why do you think you don't understand what anime is?

t_ch: 这件事说来话长…我以前是常盘国教育科学省的大臣，任期结束后，就来到匹诺康尼，研究本地的动漫产业。
t_en: It's a long story... I was once the Minister of Education and Technology in Tokiwa. Once my time in that capacity ended, I came to Penacony to research the local anime industry.

t_ch: 其实原本我只是来这里采风的，可很快却发现，这里的动漫产业和我理解中的完全不同。
t_en: I actually originally came just to perform interviews, but I quickly realized that the anime industry here was completely different to how I had understood it to be.

t_ch: 常盘国的二次元创作已经逐渐形成了一套完整的规范…该写什么样的角色、什么样的世界观、什么样的故事，早就有了成熟的模板。
t_en: Anime works in Tokiwa have gradually formed a set of complete standards and norms... A well-formed template for what kinds of characters to write, what kinds of worldviews to feature and what kinds of stories to create has been set in place for a very long time.

t_ch: 可匹诺康尼的动画和漫画似乎并非如此。这里的很多角色和情节设置，若是放到常盘国的市场里，一定会被很多人斥责「这不是二次元」！
t_en: However, animations and comics in Penacony are not this way. A lot of the characters and plot devices used here, if they were released in the Tokiwa market, would have people screaming: "This isn't anime!"

t_ch: 我本来也是抱着猎奇的心态去接触那些作品的，没想到它们有着远超我预期的想象力……
t_en: I originally kept a curious mind when I came into contact with these works. The level of imagination in them far exceeded my expectations...

t_ch: 那是一种常盘国的先贤们才有的想象力，而在如今的常盘国，早已不复得见了。
t_en: It is a level of imagination that only the sages of Tokiwa once had, one that has not been seen in Tokiwa for a long time.

t_ch: 所以…我开始动摇了。是不是我们常盘国的所谓「二次元」，已经变质成了一种无趣的定型文，而我们引以为傲的那些创作规范…也许都是错的……
t_en: So, I began to waver. Could it be that what we call "anime" in Tokiwa had long-since degraded to become a boring set of tropes, and those standards that we once held so proudly... could be incorrect...?

t_ch: 你老家是个怎么样的地方？
t_en: What is your home like?

t_ch: 常盘国是个自然资源匮乏的国家，我们在公司贸易体系中最重要的出口商品，就是我们的二次元艺术品。
t_en: Tokiwa is a country of sparse natural resources, and our most valuable export in the IPC trade system is our anime artwork.

t_ch: 所以长久以来，动漫创作是常盘国人的普遍教养。任何知识分子都会画漫画，民众也不会给没参与过动画制作的政治家投票。
t_en: So, for a long time, making anime has been part of the Tokiwa people's upbringing. Any educated person can draw manga and the people would never vote for a politician who hasn't been part of an anime's creation.

t_ch: 可随着时间的推移，我们的创作越来越故步自封，创作者对着所谓「模板」，不敢越雷池一步。
t_en: However, as time has progressed, our works have become more and more stale and creators are reluctant to step outside of this so-called "template."

t_ch: 我一度觉得这是常盘国文艺创作开始步入正轨的结果，直到来到匹诺康尼，我才恍然惊觉…自己是不是已经扼杀了无数潜在的天才？
t_en: I once believed that this was a result of Tokiwa's literary creations being on the right track. That wasn't until I came to Penacony that I suddenly realized... I might have killed the creativity of countless potential geniuses.

t_ch: 可话虽如此，但到底什么是二次元…二次元创作又到底应该遵循什么规范…我是越来越想不通了……
t_en: Saying that, the more I think about "what anime actually is" and "what standards should anime really obey"... The more lost I become...

t_ch: 我还有事，下次再聊。
t_en: I have things to take care of. Let's speak again another time.

t_ch: 好啊，下次如果有时间，可以聊聊你对二次元的理解。
t_en: Alright. If you get time, you can talk to me about your understanding of anime.

t_ch: 可以摸摸你头上的绒球吗？
t_en: Can I touch the fluff ball on your head?

t_ch: 摸了也不会变成动漫高手哦～
t_en: It won't turn you into an anime expert, you know!

t_ch: 我还有事，下次再聊。
t_en: I have things to take care of. Let's speak again another time.

t_ch: 好啊，下次如果有时间，可以聊聊你对二次元的理解。
t_en: Alright. If you get time, you can talk to me about your understanding of anime.

t_ch: 平田平次皱着眉头，嘴里念念有词，似乎正在思考母国动漫产业的未来。
t_en: Hirata Heiji furrows his brow, mumbling something to himself. It seems that he is deeply considering the future of the anime industry in his homeland...

t_ch: 是否应该彻底抛弃掉所谓「久经市场验证的创作规则」，去拥抱更加不确定的可能性呢？
t_en: Are we supposed to completely abandon these so-called "creation standards that have repeatedly proven themselves in the market," to embrace more uncertain possibilities?

t_ch: 这也许会带来更好的作品，但也可能会导致极为惨痛的失败……
t_en: This may bring higher-quality works, but it may also lead to a disastrous failure...

t_ch: 究竟该怎么做呢？真是难以抉择……
t_en: What are we supposed to do? This truly is a difficult decision...

t_ch: 我其实有些摇摆…艺术创作是否应该有所谓的规范呢？
t_en: I'm actually starting to waver... Is artistic creation even supposed to have these so-called standards?

t_ch: 如果没有规范，那艺术作品和鬼画符的区别是什么？如果有规范，那是否必然会导向如今常盘国艺术节规范呆板的现状？
t_en: If there are no standards, then what differentiates art from just random scribbling? If there are standards, then that will inevitably lead to the current situation we see in Tokiwa, with regulations and standards causing stagnation.

t_ch: 也许只有搞清楚这件事，才能为常盘国的未来找到道路。
t_en: Perhaps figuring this out is my only way to find a path for Tokiwa's future.

t_ch: 平田平次皱着眉头，眼中含着泪，似乎正为母国的动漫产业而忧心。
t_en: Hirata Heiji furrows his brow, with a tear in his eye. It seems that he is deeply concerned for the future of the anime industry in his homeland...

t_ch: 我太缺少危机意识了。常盘国自己的市场够大，在公司的贸易体系内也一直占有相当程度的份额。
t_en: I was too oblivious to our crisis. Tokiwa's market is sufficiently developed and we have always occupied considerable shares within the IPC trade system.

t_ch: 所以我一直以为，我们的动漫产业已经足够好了…直到来到匹诺康尼，我才意识到危机离我有多近。
t_en: So, I always believed that our anime industry was just fine... It wasn't until I came to Penacony that I realized how close we were to crisis.

t_ch: 我做了很多年的教育科学省大臣，非常清楚匹诺康尼动漫产业所蕴含的市场潜力…只要他们有心去做更进一步的商业化运作，那常盘国的二次元帝国必将遭受一场浩劫……
t_en: I've served as the Minister of Education and Technology for many years, so I know very well the market potential contained within the Penacony anime industry... All it takes would be for them to increase their market operations, and the Tokiwa anime empire would fall into catastrophe...

t_ch: 是时候做出改变了，因为危机总比它看起来更加迫近……
t_en: It is time for change. Crisis is always closer than it seems.

t_ch: 平田平次凝望着天空，怒目圆瞪。
t_en: Hirata Heiji stares into the sky, his eyes wide with anger.

t_ch: 我现在开始恨自己了。我真的恨。常盘国的人民选我做大臣，可我却沉浸在四海太平的假象之中，忘记了居安思危的简单道理。
t_en: I have started to hate myself. Truly, I despise myself. The citizens of Tokiwa have selected me to be their Minister, yet I allow myself to be immersed in delusions of stability. Everyone knows that it is precisely when things are peaceful that you should begin to prepare for disaster!

t_ch: 如果有一天——我想这一天也许不会太久——常盘国的二次元艺术走向了没落，那我身为教育科学省的前大臣，必然是最大的罪人！
t_en: If the day comes — A day that I don't think is far away — when Tokiwa anime falls into decline, then my time spent as the Minister of Education and Technology was my biggest sin to my people!

t_ch: 现在的受众还能够接受常盘国的二次元，只是因为他们早已习惯了！而并非因为这些真的是全银河最好的动漫产品！
t_en: The only reason that our audiences are still able to accept Tokiwa anime is because they've long-since grown used to it! It is not because our works are the best in the cosmos at all!

t_ch: 等到人们发现，在那些未被人关注到的角落，存在着无数更新更好的作品时，我那伟大的「二次元帝国」的末日就到了……
t_en: When people realize that there are countless greater works hidden in the galaxy's unexplored corners, our great anime empire will meet its day of judgment...

t_ch: 平田平次皱着眉头，但嘴角却挂着微笑。
t_en: Hirata Heiji furrows his brow, but a smile hangs at the corners of his lips.

t_ch: 其实也挺好的。虽然我遭受到了巨大的文化冲击，但这又何尝不是一种幸事呢？
t_en: You know what, that is okay, too. Although I suffered a great culture shock, is that not a good thing?

t_ch: 我是卸任官员，是政治家，是常盘国的名流——但在那些身份之前，我首先是个漫画家！
t_en: I am a retired official, a politician, and a household name across Tokiwa... But, before all these things, I am an anime artist!

t_ch: 我喜欢漫画，漫画是这个世界上最伟大的叙事艺术。但如果没有来到匹诺康尼，遭受到这莫大的冲击，我对漫画的认知恐怕会永远陷入浅薄的泥沼。
t_en: I like manga, as it is the greatest storytelling art form in existence. However, if I had never come to Penacony and experienced this shock, I fear that my knowledge of manga would have been forever stuck in the shallows.

t_ch: 我会继续考察匹诺康尼的动漫产业，学习他们的技法，研究他们的叙事…最终我会将它们与常盘国的技法融会贯通，成为独具一格的艺术！
t_en: I shall continue to reference the Penacony anime industry, learn their techniques, and research their storytelling... Finally, I will combine them with our Tokiwa techniques and become an artist like no other!

t_ch: 这是一件幸运的事，真的！经此一遭，我有信心如常盘国的那些建国先贤一样，成为伟大的漫画家！
t_en: I am truly fortunate to have this opportunity, truly! I am confident that I can become a great manga artist, just like Tokiwa's founding sages!

t_ch: 建筑不是艺术，建筑也不该是艺术。只有能力低下者才执着于「艺术」，因为他们没有运用「技术」的知能。
t_en: Architecture is not an art, nor should it be. Only those with low capability are obsessed with "art" because they do not have the capacity to utilize "technology."

t_ch: 很有道理。
t_en: Fair enough.

t_ch: 看来你听到了我的自言自语，真是羞愧。
t_en: I see you've overheard me talking to myself. How embarrassing.

t_ch: 阿锡卡的脸上并没有露出任何羞愧的神情。
t_en: Asika's face shows absolutely no signs of embarrassment.

t_ch: 很高兴你能这么想。有机生命大都热衷于歌颂某种并不实际存在的「艺术性」，来掩盖自己在技术上的无能。你与他们不同。
t_en: I'm very pleased that you feel this way. The majority of organic lifeforms love to extol and obsess over this "art" that does not even exist, so as to cover up their technological incompetence. You are not the same as them.

t_ch: 你有点极端了。
t_en: That's a little extreme.

t_ch: 看来你听到了我的自言自语，真是羞愧。
t_en: I see you've overheard me talking to myself. How embarrassing.

t_ch: 阿锡卡的脸上并没有露出任何羞愧的神情。
t_en: Asika's face shows absolutely no signs of embarrassment.

t_ch: 你会这么说，我并不意外。有机生命大都热衷于歌颂某种并不实际存在的「艺术性」，来掩盖自己在技术上的无能。
t_en: I'm not surprised that you would say this. The majority of organic lifeforms love to extol and obsess over this "art" that does not even exist, so as to cover up their technological incompetence.

t_ch: 抱歉，我的用词有些过分。我绝对没有侮辱你或其他有机生命的故意…如果你受到了冒犯，我诚恳道歉。
t_en: My apologies. My use of words was a little harsh. I do not intend to mock you or other organic lifeforms... If I have offended you, please accept my sincere apologies.

t_ch: 只是我的那些有机学生们实在太不开窍，让我这铁疙瘩憋了一肚子火，这才忍不住口出暴言。
t_en: It's just that my organic lifeform students just don't understand what I'm teaching them. It has lit a fire in my stomach, and I could not help but blurt out these harsh words.

t_ch: 「艺术」当然是很重要的，只是…「艺术」绝不是孤立存在的某种特性，而是服从于功能而自然产生的美学景观。
t_en: Of course "art" is important. It's just... There is no way that "art" exists as an independent characteristic; it is merely an aesthetic landscape that is naturally produced as the result of a function.

t_ch: 再次抱歉，我好为人师的老毛病又犯了。当然，你若是不介意，我倒是愿意再和你聊聊。
t_en: My apologies. I always end up lecturing people like this. Of course, if you do not take offense, I would like to continue our discussion.

t_ch: 那我们聊聊吧。
t_en: Then let's discuss.

t_ch: 好啊，我非常乐于与你交流。
t_en: Alright. I am very happy to communicate with you.

t_ch: 你是做什么的？
t_en: What do you do?

t_ch: 我以前是卡戎•塔尔金星系联盟的建筑系教授，现在是折纸大学筑梦技术系的教授。
t_en: I was once an architecture professor in the Charon-Targin System Alliance. Now I am a professor of dreamweaving technology at Paperfold University.

t_ch: 我平时在「太阳的时刻」上班，下班后才会来「黄金的时刻」让自己放松一下。
t_en: Normally, after I finish work in the Moment of Sol, I come to Golden Hour to relax by myself.

t_ch: 建筑师和筑梦师，这两种工作没什么本质区别。虽然在建筑手段上大有不同，但对于我们设计者来说，工作还是一样的工作。
t_en: An architect and a Dreamweaver, the two jobs have no intrinsic difference. Although there are many differences in the methods of construction, to us designers, the work is the same.

t_ch: 不过嘛…梦境终究是个更自由的地方，很多原本只能留在蓝图上的概念性建筑，完全可以在匹诺康尼得以实现。
t_en: However... The dreamscape is a place of greater freedom. Many conceptual buildings that originally would have never made it further than a blueprint are completely possible in Penacony.

t_ch: 我一向不觉得这是好事。这让很多匹诺康尼的筑梦师沾染上了「务虚」的恶习，建造出了一堆非用户友好的垃圾建筑。
t_en: I have never thought that it's a good thing. This allows Dreamweavers in Penacony to fall into the bad habits of "focusing on ideology" and constructing a load of non-user-friendly garbage structures.

t_ch: 比方说…只要触发某个开关就能调转重力、让人在墙上走？这种功能到底有什么用？花里胡哨的，很有趣吗？
t_en: For example... What if there's a switch that could turn off gravity and allow people to walk along walls? Is this function useful? Is it fun to get wrapped up in this nonsense?

t_ch: 不过恐怕光凭我自己，是很难扭转这矫饰主义的风气了。
t_en: Though I fear that it will not be easy for me to reverse this trend of pretentious aesthetics alone.

t_ch: 你的学生们很糟糕吗？
t_en: Are your students that bad?

t_ch: 糟糕？那倒不至于，他们都是很优秀的年轻人。只是…我和我的学生们，可能是相互折磨的典范。
t_en: Bad? They're not. They're all excellent young people. It's just that... my students and I may be the epitome of mutual torment.

t_ch: 我恨他们不开窍，天天去凭空追求艺术，而忽略了建筑的本质应当是实用性。他们也恨我，恨我不肯放他们自由地追寻梦想。
t_en: I hate that they spend their days chasing artistic ideas that are based on nothing and overlook the fact that the innate nature of architecture is practicality. They also hate that I will not allow them to go and freely chase their dreams.

t_ch: 我在他们眼中就是那种典型的智械教授，呆板、守旧、无法理解有机生命的追求…像从什么歧视智械的有机至上主义电影中走出来的角色一样。
t_en: In their eyes, I am just a classic Intellitron professor, inflexible, outdated and incapable of understanding the pursuits of organic lifeforms... They're like characters out of some organic-supremacist film that looks down on Intellitrons.

t_ch: 但他们不了解我，也不了解建筑学。我可能…也不够了解他们吧。
t_en: But they do not understand me, nor architecture. Perhaps... I do not understand them well enough, either.

t_ch: 唉…我现在只希望他们不要真的恨我。
t_en: Ah... I just wish that they will not truly hate me.

t_ch: 还有事，拜拜。
t_en: I have things to do, bye!

t_ch: 再见，可爱的朋友。
t_en: Farewell, my loveable friend.

t_ch: 还有事，拜拜。
t_en: I have things to do, bye!

t_ch: 再见，可爱的朋友。
t_en: Farewell, my loveable friend.

t_ch: 事实上没有任何证据表明，有机生命比无机生命「更浪漫」或是「更懂艺术」。很多人只是没办法反驳我的观点，才选择攻击我作为智械的身份。
t_en: In truth, there is no evidence that states that organic lifeforms are "more romantic" or "have a greater understanding of art" than the inorganic. Many people just choose to attack my identity as an Intellitron as they do not have a way to refute this view.

t_ch: 坦白说，我不太看得起很多「伟大」的建筑师。他们的思维方式非常诡异，充满了不合逻辑的倒果为因。
t_en: I'll be frank. I do not hold many "great" architects in high regard. They use bizarre methods of thought which are full of illogical decisions that mistake effect for cause.

t_ch: 比方说折纸大学的另一个教授，嘎鲁酋长——以前还是博识学会的呢。她就一直强调要跳脱「建筑语言的直叙性」，创造一种「比喻性的建筑语言」。
t_en: Take my fellow professor at Paperfold University, for example: Chief Gru — former member of the Intelligentsia Guild. She always stressed the need for us to break away from the "direct narrative nature of architectural language" and create a kind of "metaphorical architectural language."

t_ch: 嘎鲁酋长设计「泰科铵大球馆」时，引经据典去诠释她的艺术思想——
t_en: When Chief Gru designed the Taikiyan Stadium, she quoted classic architectural works to explain her artistic thought process.

t_ch: 「对五个琥珀纪以来极繁主义滥觞的驳斥」、「阐释了非资本性的资本竞技及其先天性的二律背反」…实在太过佶屈聱牙，我一个智械都背不出来。
t_en: "A rebuttal against the origin of maximalism for five Amber Eras," "An analysis of non-capital capital competition and their intrinsic antimony"... It was so convoluted that even an Intellitron like myself would struggle to memorize it.

t_ch: 可到最后，有人在乎她那些深奥的「艺术理论」吗？没有。人们只在乎大球馆极不合理的卫生间位置，以及缺乏安全性的球场设计。
t_en: But, in the end, did anyone care about her "artistic theories"? No. People only cared about the extremely illogical locations of the toilets within the large stadium and the unsafe design of the court.

t_ch: 这些缺陷后来都被公司擅自改掉了，嘎鲁酋长直到现在还在生气，说公司「败坏了艺术」，太可笑了。
t_en: IPC later fixed these problems themselves without consultation. Chief Gru is still mad to this day, saying that IPC ruined art. It's ridiculous.

t_ch: 其实大家对我也有所误会…我并非先天就是个呆板的老学究，我曾经也是个充满激情的建筑师。我做过那样的建筑师，所以我才知道那样是不行的。
t_en: People actually misunderstand me... I have not always been a rigid old pedant. I used to be an extremely enthusiastic young architect. It is precisely because I have lived as this kind of architect that I know it does not work.

t_ch: 好像和你说过，我在卡戎•塔尔金的时候也是大学教授。在「赫尔辛格•塔尔金纪念大学」建筑设计系任教。
t_en: I think I mentioned to you before, I was a university professor when I was in Charon-Targin. I was an instructor in the department of architectural design at Herzinger Targin Memorial University.

t_ch: 大学五百年校庆的时候，我主导设计了一个三万人大礼堂。当时我受矫饰主义建筑学派影响很深，也执着于做一些极繁的、非用户友好的建筑。
t_en: For the school's <unbreak>500</unbreak>th anniversary celebration, I led the design of an auditorium with a thirty thousand person capacity. At the time, I was heavily influenced by mannerist teachings, and I was obsessed with making overly complex and non-user-friendly buildings.

t_ch: 「赫尔辛格大礼堂」就是我当时最得意的作品。为了完成这个「伟大」的作品，我牺牲了很多建筑本应有的东西……
t_en: The Herzinger Auditorium was my proudest work at the time. However, in order to create this great work, I sacrificed a lot of elements that a building was supposed to have...

t_ch: 比如…一定程度的安全性。真的只是牺牲了一点点安全性，一点点而已。我又不是疯子，不可能完全罔顾安全。
t_en: For example... a set level of safety. It really was just a little safety precaution that I sacrificed, just a tiny one... I am not a madman, so I would never completely forsake safety.

t_ch: 可就是这一点点被牺牲的安全性，最终导致了近两万名师生…唉…我说不下去了，你自己查吧，「赫尔辛格大礼堂坍塌事故」。有不少关于这个的纪录片。
t_en: But just a little sacrifice in safety led to nearly twenty thousand students and teachers... Ah... I can't bring myself to talk about it. You can look it up for yourself: "The disaster of the Herzinger Auditorium collapse." There are a number of documentaries detailing it.

t_ch: 事发之后，很多人为我辩护。他们说这些不是我的错，他们说这是施工方的问题，他们说这是选材的失误…可我很清楚，这就是我的错。
t_en: After the incident, many people came to my defense. They said that it was not my fault; they said that it was the construction company's fault, and they'd made a mistake when selecting materials... But I knew very well, that this was my mistake.

t_ch: 从那以后，我就恨透了我自己，也恨透了我那时迷信的「建筑艺术」…我们合谋，害死了很多很多人……
t_en: Since then, I have hated myself to my core and hated this blind worship of the "art of architecture"... This path we plotted killed so many people.

t_ch: 我其实每天都想从折纸大学辞职。梦境里的人比外面的人还务虚，有时候我都想一人赏他们一闷棍，再拖到小黑屋里打到他们放弃梦想为止。
t_en: If I'm honest, I think about quitting my job at Paperfold University every day. People in the dreamscape are even more unrealistic than those outside. Sometimes, I want to beat them for their ideas, then drag them into a dark room and beat them until they abandon their dreams entirely.

t_ch: 我这话不好听吧？但我这可是真心话！哎…我发自内心希望他们能明白，他们的阿锡卡教授绝不是无法理解他们的「艺术」！
t_en: I imagine that sounds terrible? But it is my truth! Ah... I truly wish that they will come to understand, it is not that their Professor Asika could never understand their "art"!

t_ch: 但如果想搞纯艺术，就去艺术系吧！筑梦技术系不需要那么多务虚的人！
t_en: But if they just wish to focus on art, they should become artists! The dream construction department does not need all these unrealistic Dreamweavers!

t_ch: 到底什么是「建筑的艺术」？非常简单！就一句话——「建筑是一种机器」！
t_en: Just what is the "art of architecture"? It's simple! "A building is a type of machine"!

t_ch: 那，什么是「机器的艺术」？最美的飞船长成什么样子？易于航行的外表设计、坚固安全的外壳选材、低耗高效的虚数内能引擎…这样的飞船是最实用的，也是最美的，不是吗？
t_en: Then, what is the "art of machinery"? What does the most beautiful spaceship look like? An exterior design that eases navigation, a body constructed from a sturdy and safe material, a low-emission and high-efficiency imaginary intra-kinetic engine... A ship like this is the most practical and the most beautiful, is it not?

t_ch: 飞船可能对你来说太复杂了，那说点你更熟悉的——马桶。我虽然没用过，但我知道，一个马桶最重要的是什么？舒适、清洁、便利。设想一个完美践行实用性原则的马桶吧！
t_en: Perhaps a spaceship is too complicated for you. Let me talk about something you're more familiar with — a toilet. Though I have never used one, I understand what the most important facets of a toilet are. Comfort, cleanliness and convenience. Let's imagine the perfect toilet that implements the principles of practicality!

t_ch: 它有符合人体工程学的流线型座位，像王座一样舒服。它表面喷涂着惰性生物瓷，永远无臭又干净。它与卫生间的墙壁融为一体，毫无卫生死角。
t_en: It would have an ergonomic, streamlined seat that offers the comfort of a throne. Its surface would be coated with an inert bio-porcelain that is always free of odors and dirt. It would be fused into the bathroom wall to leave no corners for bacteria to thrive.

t_ch: 这样一款马桶的设计者，估计脑子里从来都没想过「艺术性」这三个字，但这款马桶，毫无疑问是真正的艺术品。它在美学意义上绝对是完美的！
t_en: I imagine that the designer of such a toilet would never have the word "artistic" cross their mind, yet this toilet would without a doubt be a work of art. In terms of aesthetics, it is undoubtedly perfect!

t_ch: 建筑是一种机器！一切符合生物使用需求的东西，都自然而然是美的，而绝不需要有人去倒果为因，凭空追寻艺术！他们追寻的所谓艺术，无非是「真空中的球形鸡」罢了！
t_en: Buildings are a type of machine! Something that is completely suited to use by organics is naturally beautiful. There is no need for people to flip the process on its head in a meaningless search for artistic beauty! This "art" that they pursue is merely some unrealistic hypothesis!

t_ch: 那些可悲的建筑大师们，苦心孤诣一辈子所构想出来的「建筑艺术」，还远远不如一个马桶！
t_en: The "art of architecture" that these pitiful architectural masters have spent their entire lives striving to conceive is not even equal to a simple toilet!

t_ch: 我现在完全是凭着一腔热血，才继续在折纸大学任教的。我现在根本没办法从工作中找到什么乐趣，唯有来黄金的时刻散心时，才能久违地感到些快乐。
t_en: I am only able to continue my teaching position at Paperfold University due to this burning passion. I find no joy in my work anymore. It is only when I come to Golden Hour to relax that I can feel some of this long-lost happiness.

t_ch: 说真的我还是喜欢设计建筑的。我喜欢做建筑师，也喜欢做筑梦师。
t_en: Honestly, I still enjoy designing buildings. I like being an architect and a Dreamweaver.

t_ch: 我也绝对不是反对人们追求「艺术」，只是反对建筑师和筑梦师们普遍性地搞错重点。
t_en: I am also in no way opposed to humans pursuing "art." I am just opposed to architects and Dreamweavers widely mistaking what is truly important.

t_ch: 应该牢记一个准则——「形式永远服从功能」。只要践行这个准则，功能性自然会带来形式上的美。我还从来没见过任何一个建筑物是「实用但丑陋」的。
t_en: There is one principle that should be remembered at all times — "Form must always follow function." If you can realize this principle, functionality will naturally bring out the beauty of the form. I have never once seen a building that was "functional but ugly."

t_ch: 说实话，作为智械，我的脾气实在是太差了，有时候不愿意好好和学生们沟通。如果我们能好好沟通，也许他们也能明白我的良苦用心。
t_en: In truth, as an Intellitron, I have a terrible temper and sometimes I am unwilling to properly communicate with my students. If we could communicate properly, perhaps they would understand that I mean well.

t_ch: 唉…等哪次上课时，我心情像今天这么好的时候，应该能好好和他们聊聊这个话题吧。
t_en: Ah... Perhaps one day in class, when I am in as fine a mood as I am now, I should be able to discuss this topic properly with them.

t_ch: 可惜一到学校，我的电压就开始升高，估计是很难有这种机会了，哈哈。
t_en: It is just a shame that the moment I arrive at the school, my voltage starts to rise and I imagine it will not be easy to find such an opportunity, haha.

t_ch: 您好，请问有什么需要帮助的吗？
t_en: Hello, is there anything that I can help you with?

t_ch: 我是隶属「猎犬」的奥希洛特，负责这片区域的安保工作。
t_en: I am Auslotte of the Bloodhound Family. I am responsible for security in this area.

t_ch: 你在看什么？
t_en: What are you looking at?

t_ch: …我只是在日常巡逻而已。您有什么事吗？
t_en: ...I am just on my daily patrol. Can I help you with something?

t_ch: 你在盯着…波奇？
t_en: You're staring at... Bocchi?

t_ch: 看来您认识这位…波奇先生，那么我是否该将您也纳入监控名单呢？
t_en: I see you know this... Mr. Bocchi. Should I then add you to my list of surveillance targets?

t_ch: 我其实和他不熟…
t_en: I don't know him that well...

t_ch: 如果是这样的话，就请您不要妨碍我执行公务。以及……
t_en: If that is the case, then I would ask you not to interfere with my professional duties. What's more...

t_ch: 为了您个人的安全着想，请尽量减少与波奇先生，以及他身边那位老先生的接触。
t_en: Out of consideration for your personal safety, please make sure to limit your interaction with Mr. Bocchi and that old guy around him.

t_ch: 可以摸摸你头上的绒球吗？
t_en: Can I touch the fluff ball on your head?

t_ch: …很好， 看来您也是我接下来需要重点关注的对象了。
t_en: ...Very well. It seems that you are also a target that I shall have to focus on in the future.

t_ch: 根据您轻浮的态度与无理的要求来推断，只怕早已是骚扰皮皮西的惯犯了吧。
t_en: Judging from your carefree attitude and irrational requests, I fear that you may already be a repeat offender when it comes to harassing the Pepeshi people.

t_ch: 您放心，我会尽快完成调查取证，让您得到应有的处置……
t_en: Rest assured. I shall complete my investigation quickly and find my conclusive evidence, so that you shall be dealt with as you deserve.

t_ch: 等等，我就开个玩笑…
t_en: Wait! Wait! I was just joking...

t_ch: 我只是有点好奇那个球！
t_en: I'm just a little curious about that ball!

t_ch: …既然这样，请允许我郑重告诫您——此类言行对于皮皮西一族而言极为失礼，请切勿再犯。
t_en: ...If that is the case, allow me to give you a severe warning — such speech and actions are considered extremely disrespectful to the Pepeshi people. Please refrain from doing this again.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 愿您在匹诺康尼度过一段愉快的时光。
t_en: I hope that you have a pleasant time in Penacony.

t_ch: 您好，请问有什么需要帮助的吗？
t_en: Hello, is there anything that I can help you with?

t_ch: 我是隶属「猎犬」的奥希洛特，负责这片区域的安保工作。
t_en: I am Auslotte of the Bloodhound Family. I am responsible for security in this area.

t_ch: 麻烦死了，啧……
t_en: This is a real pain, tsk...

t_ch: 真见鬼，这两个家伙是怎么凑到一块儿去的？
t_en: How did this happen? How did these two end up together?

t_ch: 一个诈骗嫌疑犯还不够，现在又来了个星际通缉的纵火犯。
t_en: As if a fraudster alone isn't enough, now I've got an interastrally wanted arsonist.

t_ch: 头儿怎么总是把这种麻烦的活派给我啊……
t_en: Why does the boss keep giving me the worst gigs...

t_ch: 皮皮西那张严肃的小脸上浮现出几分疲惫。
t_en: You see a vague sense of exhaustion sweep across the stern face of the Pepeshi person.

t_ch: 假期…我的假期…又泡汤了！
t_en: My holiday... My vacation... Gone to waste again!

t_ch: 我都记不清自己已经连续加班多久了…早该想个办法加入苜蓿草了，唉……
t_en: I can't even think how much overtime I've worked back-to-back now... I should have figured out a way to join The Alfalfa Family...

t_ch: 皮皮西的眼睛闪闪发亮——尽管她仍在试图绷紧自己的脸色。
t_en: The Pepeshi's eyes shone brightly, despite her attempts to keep her face rigid and serious.

t_ch: 她…她又邀请我去音乐咖啡厅了……
t_en: She... She's invited me to the music café again...

t_ch: 咳、其实我也没有多期待和她共进晚餐，只不过是想去尝尝那家的新品罢了……
t_en: Ahem, well, it's not that I'm that excited to have dinner with her. I'm just looking forward to trying their new menu...

t_ch: 尊敬的宾客，欢迎来到「白日梦」酒店。请问有什么可以为您效劳的吗？
t_en: Welcome to The Reverie, dear guest. How may I assist you?

t_ch: 想再了解下我的客房信息。
t_en: I'd like to learn about my room.

t_ch: 好的。作为家族的贵客，您当前的房间位于「红心十二区」<unbreak>666</unbreak>号白金套房内。
t_en: Certainly. Since you are a valued guest of The Family, your room is Platinum Suite <unbreak>666</unbreak>, located in Zone <unbreak>12</unbreak> of the Red Heart.

t_ch: 本酒店套房为多层密闭结构，每间套房内包含一个独立的会客厅，全天候提供酒水、演奏等服务。
t_en: Our suites feature multi-story and well-sealed structures. Each suite contains a separate parlor and provides <unbreak>24</unbreak>/7 drink and performance service.

t_ch: 入住期间，如有换房、提前退房等需求，请通过您的「梦境护照」递交申请，我们将在一个工作日内为您办理完毕。
t_en: If you need to change rooms or check out, please submit your request via your Dreamscape Pass, and we will process it within one working day.

t_ch: 关于之前的风波…
t_en: About the previous incident...

t_ch: 万分抱歉为您带来了不便的体验。我们会进一步优化工作流程，提升服务质量，以保证您能够充分享受美梦之旅。
t_en: We apologize for any inconvenience you may have experienced. We will refine our workflow and enhance our service quality to ensure that you can fully enjoy your trip.

t_ch: 我没事了。
t_en: That's all.

t_ch: 请别客气，有关酒店的任何问题我都乐意为您解答。为您服务是全体「家族」成员的荣幸。
t_en: You're welcome. I'm ready to answer any questions you may have about the hotel. It is an honor for all The Family's members to serve you.

t_ch: 祝您在匹诺康尼度过一段愉快的时光。
t_en: We wish you a pleasant stay in Penacony.

t_ch: 尊敬的宾客，欢迎来到「白日梦」酒店。请问有什么可以为您效劳的吗？
t_en: Welcome to The Reverie, dear guest. How may I assist you?

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 当然，您有什么想要咨询的？
t_en: Certainly. Is there anything specific you would like to know about?

t_ch: 为我介绍一下酒店吧。
t_en: Tell me more about this hotel.

t_ch: 好的。「白日梦酒店」是匹诺康尼的标志性建筑，也是现实与梦境的中转站。
t_en: Certainly. The Reverie is a renowned landmark in Penacony, serving as a transit point between reality and Dreamscape.

t_ch: 每位想要前往「梦想之城」的逐梦客都需要在这里办理入住手续，并由此进入匹诺康尼宏伟瑰丽的梦境世界。
t_en: All Dreamchasers intending to enter the City of Dreams need to check in here before entering the magnificent dream world in Penacony.

t_ch: 酒店依照朝向、楼层划分为<unbreak>48</unbreak>个扇区。您的房间位于「红心十二区」<unbreak>666</unbreak>号白金套房内。
t_en: This hotel is divided into <unbreak>48</unbreak> sub sectors, and your room, Platinum Suite <unbreak>666</unbreak>, is located in Zone <unbreak>12</unbreak> of the Red Heart.

t_ch: 入梦须知已记录在您的梦境护照上，您可以随时查阅。如有其他需求，可随时通过房间内的电话联系酒店工作人员。
t_en: Please refer to your Dreamscape Pass for instructions. Should you have any additional needs, feel free to contact our staff using the phone in your room.

t_ch: 你可以提供什么服务？
t_en: What services do you provide?

t_ch: 本窗口提供客房相关业务的办理。
t_en: This window provides all services related to your room.

t_ch: 入住期间，如有换房、提前退房等需求，请通过您的「梦境护照」递交申请，我们将在一个工作日内为您办理完毕。
t_en: If you need to change rooms or check out, please submit your request via your Dreamscape Pass, and we will process it within one working day.

t_ch: 这里使用什么货币？
t_en: What currency is used here?

t_ch: 匹诺康尼当前通行货币为「苜蓿币」。进入梦境后。您账户中的信用点将自动等比兑换为苜蓿币。
t_en: The prevailing currency in Penacony is Alfalfa credits. Upon entering Dreamscape, the credits in your account will be automatically converted to Alfalfa credits at an equal exchange rate.

t_ch: 在梦中，您可使用您的「梦境护照」账户直接进行线上交易，十分便捷。
t_en: While in Dreamscape, you can conveniently use your Dreamscape Pass for online transactions.

t_ch: 没什么想了解的了。
t_en: There's nothing else I'd like to ask about.

t_ch: 请别客气，有关酒店的任何问题我都乐意为您解答。为您服务是全体「家族」成员的荣幸。
t_en: You're welcome. I'm ready to answer any questions you may have about the hotel. It is an honor for all The Family's members to serve you.

t_ch: 感谢你的解答，我先走了。
t_en: Thank you for your answers. I'll take my leave now.

t_ch: 祝您在匹诺康尼度过一段愉快的时光。
t_en: We wish you a pleasant stay in Penacony.

t_ch: …哼哼哼♪…哼、哼哼哼……
t_en: Hmhmhm♪... Hm... Hmhmhm...

t_ch: 你望向钢琴，最先看到的却是那双手——
t_en: When your gaze falls on the piano, the first thing that captures your attention is a pair of hands...

t_ch: 那双手保养得当、指节修长，起落间漾开黑白分明的薄浪。
t_en: Well-maintained and adorned with slender knuckles, they flutter and leap, creating waves of black and white.

t_ch: 薄浪时断时续、如诉如泣；于是你看见铅灰色音符沉入水底，寂寥又美丽。
t_en: The waves carry intermittent whispers, and you observe the gray notes submerging into the water, lonesomely yet elegantly.

t_ch: 音符引领你越坠越深。而后你仰头，终于得见钢琴家其人——
t_en: Deeply captivated, you follow the notes deeper and deeper. Finally, you look up and catch a glimpse of the pianist himself...

t_ch: 他垂颈弓背、摇头晃脑、昏昏欲坠，似乎全凭一双手扎根琴键之上。
t_en: His neck is bowed, his head swaying as if he has entered a trance, rooted to the keys with only his hands.

t_ch: 于是你继续追寻那些音符。你们一路相伴，穿越沉黑的湖底、坠入深蓝的永夜、跌进冷白的孤月……
t_en: And so, you continue to chase after his musical notes. Together, you traverse the darkness at the bottom of the lake, the deep blue of the eternal night, and the cold white of the lonesome moon...

t_ch: 不知不觉之间，一曲终了——
t_en: Before you know it, the music reaches its conclusion...

t_ch: 你蓦然惊醒，徒留音符与它的造主，继续着他们不知休止的旅途。
t_en: Abruptly, you awaken, leaving the notes and their creator to continue their eternal journey.

t_ch: 钢琴家垂颈弓背、摇头晃脑、昏昏欲坠，似乎全凭一双手扎根琴键之上。
t_en: The pianist, with his neck bowed, his head swaying as if he has entered a trance, is rooted to the keys with only his hands.

t_ch: 你追寻着由他创造出的那些音符——你们一路相伴，穿越沉黑的湖底、坠入深蓝的永夜、跌进冷白的孤月……
t_en: You chase after the musical notes he has created. Together, you traverse the darkness at the bottom of the lake, the deep blue of the eternal night, and the cold white of the lonesome moon...

t_ch: 安静地继续欣赏。
t_en: Immerse yourself in the melody.

t_ch: 你听到了演奏者的咕哝声。
t_en: You notice faint hums escaping the pianist as he plays.

t_ch: …哼哼哼♪…哼、哼哼哼……
t_en: Hmhmhm♪... Hm... Hmhmhm...

t_ch: 他似乎格外钟情这段旋律，总在音符起落的间隙，见缝插针地轻哼几句。
t_en: He seems to have a particular fondness for this melody, occasionally humming a few notes between the rise and fall of his orchestration.

t_ch: 起初你不明白这究竟有何意义，但渐渐这段简单的、与周围气氛格格不入的旋律渗透你的皮肤、浸润你的神经、最终在你脑内晕开——
t_en: At first, the significance eludes you, but gradually, this simple yet out-of-place melody seeps into your mind, saturating your senses, and eventually unfolding in your mind...

t_ch: 原来那种感受叫做*孤独*。
t_en: *Loneliness*, that is the emotion that resonates within you.

t_ch: 试图吸引其注意。
t_en: Try to capture his attention.

t_ch: 你靠近舞台，清了清嗓子——钢琴家没反应；
t_en: You approach the stage and clear your throat, but the pianist remains unresponsive.

t_ch: 你又大幅度掸了掸衣摆、跺了跺脚——演奏者依旧忘我；
t_en: You flick your sleeves and stomp your foot dramatically, yet the pianist remains unaware of your presence.

t_ch: 你张了张嘴，但最终还是没有大喊出声——并不是崇高的道德突然充盈你的心灵，而是你觉得这么做并没有用。
t_en: You open your mouth but ultimately decide against shouting anything, not because you realize your rudeness, but because you realize that it won't work.

t_ch: 钢琴家正沉浸于自己的世界中。那个世界和谐圆融、自成一体，就如同天体运行、潮汐涨落，而你无法搅扰干涉。
t_en: The pianist is immersed in his own world, a realm of harmony and abundance, akin to the celestial bodies in motion and the ebb and flow of the tides — something beyond your disruption.

t_ch: 悄然离开。
t_en: Leave in silence.

t_ch: …哼哼哼♪…哼、哼哼哼……
t_en: Hmhmhm♪... Hm... Hmhmhm...

t_ch: 演奏者继续忘我地弹奏着、哼唱着，并未发现某位萍水相逢的观众已悄然离去。
t_en: The pianist continues to play and hum, oblivious to the fact that his audience has quietly left.

t_ch: 您好，{F#美丽的女士}{M#英俊的先生}，能与您这样{F#美丽}{M#俊朗}的贵宾相遇，是我的荣幸。请让我为您调一杯饮品吧——
t_en: Greetings, {F#beautiful lady}{M#handsome sir}. It is an honor to meet such a {F#beautiful}{M#handsome} guest as yourself. Allow me to prepare a drink for you.

t_ch: 嗯…朗姆和苏打、酸橙、薄荷与糖砂，在那位著名龙裔巡海游侠的方子上做些小小的改良，就成了一杯独属于您的「{RUBY_B#莫吉托}星间旅人{RUBY_E#}」。
t_en: Well, this recipe is improved based on that famous Galaxy Ranger's recipe... rum, soda, lime, mint, and a touch of sugar, and you will get the unique mix: {RUBY_B#Mojito}Galactic Voyager{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 听着还不错！
t_en: Sounds good!

t_ch: 哈哈，我就知道您会喜欢……
t_en: Haha, I had a feeling you would enjoy it...

t_ch: 太普通了吧？
t_en: Isn't it too ordinary?

t_ch: 哦？倒是我小瞧您了…那么这个呢——卡利白、柠檬青，金松子、橙水晶，再辅以少许苦艾与茴香，我叫它「重生者」。
t_en: Oh, my apologies... How about this? Blended with Kapo-Kali, Lemon Lazuli, golden pine nut, and orange crystal, with a subtle touch of absinthe and anise? I call it "Rebirth."

t_ch: 但我还没成年…
t_en: But I'm not an adult yet...

t_ch: …哦，只看外表还真看不出来…我这就给您更换饮品。
t_en: Oh... my apologies... Let me prepare a more suitable drink for you.

t_ch: 这是匹诺康尼特产——经典苏乐达，您请。
t_en: Here, a Penacony specialty — Classic SoulGlad, if you please.

t_ch: 我瞧您还是生面孔…刚刚才来到匹诺康尼吗？
t_en: I see you are not quite accustomed to this place... Are you new to Penacony?

t_ch: 如果不急着入梦，您倒是可以常来坐坐。这吧台虽小，但舞台很大——
t_en: If you are not in a rush to enter the dream world, please feel free to frequent here. This small bar has a huge stage...

t_ch: 这人来人往、熙熙攘攘，可谓是信息的川流、故事的沃壤。
t_en: With so many customers here, this place is a vast repository of information and a fertile ground for stories.

t_ch: 如果您有什么想了解、却又不知该从何开始了解的，不妨来这里点上一杯——一杯饮品换一个故事，保证让您满意而归。
t_en: If you have questions or are seeking information, please come here and order a drink — a drink for a story. I guarantee my stories will satisfy your curiosity.

t_ch: 当然啦，您要是自信自己的故事足够精彩，可以被用来交换酒水…那我也求之不得。
t_en: And of course, if you have splendid stories to exchange for drinks... I'd be delighted to hear them.

t_ch: 这杯就当是我送您的见面礼吧，不收钱——记得以后常来。
t_en: Here, this is on the house. Don't forget to come here often.

t_ch: 您好啊，{F#美丽的女士}{M#英俊的先生}，今天想要喝点什么呢？
t_en: Greetings, {F#beautiful lady}{M#handsome sir}. What would you like today?

t_ch: 有什么推荐饮品？
t_en: Any recommended beverage?

t_ch: 从个人角度出发，我的回答是：每个人都有一杯属于自己的「独家特饮」。
t_en: Well, I believe each guest has their own "exclusive special drink."

t_ch: 不过作为酒店的调饮师，还请容我向您推荐一款最入门、但也最值得细细品味的饮品——原味苏乐达。
t_en: However, as the bartender, I would like to recommend the most popular yet enjoyable option, the Classic SoulGlad.

t_ch: 就算是为了「合群」，您也应当尝尝这种气泡糖浆。
t_en: I recommend trying this concoction, even if it's just to blend in.

t_ch: 你很喜欢八卦？
t_en: Do you enjoy gossiping?

t_ch: 这个…您不觉得调酒的过程非常有趣吗——
t_en: Well... don't you find the process of mixing drinks interesting?

t_ch: 将那些相干或不相干的基酒原材，通过特殊手法、以特定比例调配，就能得到千奇百怪的有趣饮品。
t_en: Combine base ingredients, both related and unrelated, in specific proportions to create a variety of intriguing beverages.

t_ch: 我喜欢研究这种让简单朴素的材料发生「质变」的规律与方法。我觉得这个过程非常…迷人。
t_en: To transform common ingredients into something extraordinary through certain rules and techniques... I find this process... fascinating.

t_ch: 而您所谓的「八卦」——或者说人们带来的信息与故事——也可以作为调酒的原料被加工、混合。很多时候，它们能带来比枯燥的香料更浓厚的韵味。
t_en: Therefore, what some may refer to as "gossip," or rather, the information and stories, can be treated as ingredients and mixed, gaining a richer flavor compared to mundane spices.

t_ch: 为什么不去梦里调酒？
t_en: Why aren't you bartending in the Dreamscape?

t_ch: 哈…好问题。我虽是名调饮师，却并不享受「迷醉」的感觉。
t_en: Hah... a great question. While I'm a bartender, I don't particularly enjoy the feeling of indulgence.

t_ch: 人们总喜欢沉迷于短暂而不真实的欢愉，酒是这样、梦也是如此。
t_en: People tend to indulge in fleeting, unreal pleasures, whether it's through wine or dreams.

t_ch: 但说实话，这对我而言反倒缺乏吸引力。更何况…人们来到「{RUBY_B#匹诺康尼}梦想之城{RUBY_E#}」往往是为了追求自己想要、却在现实里得不到的东西——而我？我很满意自己现在的生活。
t_en: But to be honest, it doesn't appeal to me... People come to {RUBY_B#Penacony}the City of Dreams{RUBY_E#} to pursue what they cannot attain in reality. As for me, I am already content with my current life.

t_ch: 这杯送您，尊敬的客人——希望您也能和我一样，享受知足带来的快乐。
t_en: Please take your drink, esteemed guest. I hope you find as much happiness in contentment as I do.

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 慢走，欢迎您随时再来。
t_en: Take care. You are always welcome here.

t_ch: 先走了，回见。
t_en: See you next time.

t_ch: 随时恭候您的光临。
t_en: Looking forward to seeing you next time.

t_ch: 看看你这里都有什么。
t_en: Let's see what you have here.

t_ch: 瞧啊，这不是那位把大堂闹了个底朝天的大人物嘛？来来来，咱们赶紧认识一下！
t_en: Hey, aren't you that big shot who caused a commotion in the lobby? Let's get acquainted!

t_ch: …我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: ...I'm {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}？好名字，我会牢牢记在心里的。
t_en: {NICKNAME}? A great name. I'll keep it in mind.

t_ch: 我乃银河球棒侠。
t_en: I'm Galactic Baseballer.

t_ch: 银河球棒侠？好名字啊，又高贵又独特，和您非常般配！
t_en: Galactic Baseballer? What an impressive name! Noble and unique, perfectly suited to you!

t_ch: 我很出名吗？
t_en: Am I famous?

t_ch: 哎呦，我虽不认识您，但您这长相、这气度，怎么可能是一般人呢！
t_en: I may not know you personally, but your appearance and demeanor convey to me that you are no ordinary person!

t_ch: 我是萨宾娜，来自雅利洛八号的公主。能见到您可真是三生有幸。
t_en: I'm Sabrina, Princess of Jarilo-VIII. It's an honor to meet you.

t_ch: 雅利洛…八号？
t_en: Jarilo... VIII?

t_ch: 哎哟，我们那地方是一颗偏远星球，您没听过真是太正常了。
t_en: Oh, it's a remote planet, and it's understandable if you have never heard of it.

t_ch: 但雅利洛总共只有六颗行星……
t_en: But there are only six planets in Jarilo...

t_ch: 呃…我那是…一颗卫星，对，是环绕雅利洛六号的卫星！
t_en: Um... I mean... it's actually a moon! Yeah, a moon orbiting Jarilo-VI!

t_ch: 那你一定听说过布洛妮娅了？
t_en: You must have heard of Bronya, right?

t_ch: 卜萝…泥巴…？听着耳熟，是哪位名人来着……
t_en: Bron... Naya? Sounds familiar. Who is that person?

t_ch: 你们王室姓什么？
t_en: What's your royal family's last name?

t_ch: 呃，我想想…是皮戈家族。对，皮戈！我的全名是萨宾娜•皮戈。
t_en: Last name, let me see... Oh, it's Piggot! Yeah, my full name is Sabrina Piggot!

t_ch: 那你听说过布洛妮娅•兰德吗？
t_en: Have you heard of Bronya Rand?

t_ch: …呃，好吧，我得承认…这其实是个小小的玩笑，对！就是那种初见时用来活跃气氛的玩笑。
t_en: ...Well, you know... this is just a little joke! You know? The kind of joke to break the ice when meeting a stranger.

t_ch: 不过我虽然不是公主，却也并非随便什么阿猫阿狗——我是个来自亚伯南安星的商人，在宇宙富豪榜上排名没有一百也有五千。
t_en: While I'm not a princess, I'm far from a nobody. I'm a merchant from Abelane, and I'm within the top <unbreak>5,000</unbreak>, or maybe even top <unbreak>100</unbreak> wealthiest individuals in the entire universe.

t_ch: 我此次前来匹诺康尼，就是为了结识一些门户相当、志同道合的朋友。
t_en: I've come to Penacony to make some new friends who share similar interests and backgrounds.

t_ch: 您身边要是有什么爱好交际，而且像咱们这样有身份、有地位的朋友，可别忘了给我介绍介绍啊~
t_en: If you have any friends who enjoy socializing and hold a similar status and position to us, don't hesitate to introduce me to them!

t_ch: 哎，这不是…那位{M#先生}{F#女士}嘛，再见到您真是太高兴了！
t_en: Hey, if it isn't... that kind {M#sir}{F#lady}? Great to see you again!

t_ch: 所以我是谁？
t_en: So, what's my name?

t_ch: 您就是那位啊，我们之前见过的，记得吗？
t_en: Of course I know! You are... the individual we met before, right?

t_ch: 你根本没记住我的名字。
t_en: You haven't remembered my name at all.

t_ch: …哎哟，就凭您这身打扮，任谁看上一眼都会印象深刻的。
t_en: ...Well, you see... Your outfit is so striking that it practically introduces you.

t_ch: 不过这酒店每天人来人往的，要记住每个人的名字确实有些力不从心…能劳烦您再说一遍吗？
t_en: To be honest, with the flow of guests in this hotel, it's challenging to remember everyone's name... Could you please remind me of yours?

t_ch: 我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I'm {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 哦，{NICKNAME}，多美的名字！我会牢牢记在心里的。
t_en: Oh, {NICKNAME}, what a lovely name! I'll make sure to remember it this time.

t_ch: …你觉得我还会相信吗？
t_en: ...Do you really expect me to believe that?

t_ch: 呃…啊哈哈，那就祝您有美好的一天吧。
t_en: Well... Ahaha, well then, have a pleasant day.

t_ch: 关于酒店的宾客…
t_en: About the guests in this hotel...

t_ch: 哎呦，您可算是问对人了！我整日待在这酒店大堂，来来往往的人群看得多了。
t_en: You've come to the right person! I've been in the lobby, observing the guests all day!

t_ch: 什么人出身富贵，什么人白手起家，什么人是花架子——只消一眼，我就知道他是鱼目还是珍珠！
t_en: I can easily discern who's wealthy, who's not, and who's trying to show off! Just one glance, and I can tell whether they're a gem or a grain of sand!

t_ch: 你为什么不入梦？
t_en: Why don't you enter the Dreamscape?

t_ch: 这…这当然是…当然是梦里哪有这大堂方便交朋友啊！
t_en: I—It's because... Well, making friends here, in this lobby, is much more convenient than trying to do it in the dream!

t_ch: 人一旦入了梦，长相可以变化、身份可以造假——你甚至不知道对方到底什么种族、是男是女！这种朋友我哪敢随随便便交啊……
t_en: Those people in the dream can easily change their looks and even identity! And you never know their true race or gender! You surely don't want friends like that, right?...

t_ch: 你脑中突然响起一道玄妙的无声之音——「灵感鉴定：成功」。
t_en: Suddenly, a mysterious voice echoes in your mind, "Insight check: Successful."

t_ch: 该不会是为了省钱吧？
t_en: Is it about saving money?

t_ch: …怎、怎么可能！我、我可是来自安…南安亚伯星的商人，在宇宙富豪榜上排名没有五百也有一千！
t_en: ...How, how could it be! I... I'm a successful merchant from Ab... Abenela! And I'm within the top <unbreak>1,000</unbreak>, or maybe even top <unbreak>500</unbreak> wealthiest individuals in the entire universe!

t_ch: 「南安亚伯」？你出色的记忆力悄悄提醒你，她上次说的分明是「亚伯南安」……
t_en: Abenela? Your keen memory reminds you that her previous claim was "Abelane"...

t_ch: 宇宙中真的有这颗星星吗？你不禁开始猜测——或许有，但「富商」的身份恐怕就和那个「雅利洛八号的公主」一样，都是她编造出来的故事罢了。
t_en: Does such a star really exist? You can't help but wonder. Perhaps it does, but her identity as a "wealthy merchant" is just as fabricated as her claim of being a "Princess of Jarilo-VIII."

t_ch: …您、您怎么不说话啦？该不会是饿了吧？我记得今天餐厅有「仰望星空」切片蛋糕，你…您要不先去吃一些吧？
t_en: ...Wh—Why aren't you saying anything? Are you hungry? The restaurant has the Stargazer slice cakes today. Why don't you... go and grab some?

t_ch: 她在紧张，那紧张中还带着些许窘迫与尴尬。
t_en: She looks nervous, with a tinge of embarrassment and awkwardness.

t_ch: 明明就连「忘记交谈对象的名字」这种事，都没能对她造成如此显而易见的影响——或许你真的在无意中戳中了她的痛处。
t_en: Clearly, even something significant as "forgetting the name of the person you're talking to" has a less noticeable impact on her. Perhaps you unintentionally hit a nerve.

t_ch: …我…哎呦，我突然想起等会还有个约会，就先不奉陪啦？
t_en: I... Oh... I just remembered that I have an appointment to attend, so I'll take my leave, alright?

t_ch: 先聊到这吧。
t_en: That's enough chit-chat for now.

t_ch: 嗯、嗯，再见，期待我们的下一次谈话~
t_en: Yeah, yeah... I look forward to our next conversation.

t_ch: 身份判断：酒店贵宾。
t_en: Identity confirmed: Esteemed guest.

t_ch: 欢迎来到匹诺康尼，贵宾。愿您在梦境中度过一段愉快的时光。
t_en: Welcome to Penacony, esteemed guest. We wish you a pleasant stay in the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 我是入梦护理师总管，乔凡娜。如果您需要任何帮助，欢迎随时与我交流。
t_en: I am Giovanna, the Chief Dream Nurse. If you require any assistance, please don't hesitate to approach me.

t_ch: 我有问题！
t_en: I do need your assistance!

t_ch: 好的，请您详述问题内容。
t_en: Acknowledged. Please provide more details.

t_ch: 「入梦」是什么？
t_en: What does a "dream nurse" do?

t_ch: 根据字面意义，入梦即进入梦境。
t_en: Well, "dream" refers to the process of entering the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 酒店每间客房内都配备特殊「入梦池」，引导客人前往指定梦境。
t_en: Each room is equipped with a specialized "Dreampool" to guide our esteemed guests into the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 家族会为您提供最正规、最安全的服务，以及最完美的梦境体验。
t_en: The Family offers the most secure authorized service, along with a flawless dream experience.

t_ch: 还有非正规入梦吗？
t_en: So, are there "unauthorized" services?

t_ch: 近日有位蓝发「商人」四处兜售「价格低廉，功能可靠」的新式入梦工具。
t_en: Well, recently, a blue-haired "merchant" has been selling a new type of "affordable and reliable" dreaming device.

t_ch: 目前受害者已达十余人，猎犬已经介入调查，但嫌疑人尚未被逮捕归案。
t_en: Over ten individuals have fallen victim to this scam. The Bloodhounds are investigating, but the suspect remains at large.

t_ch: 此外，请勿轻信「量子穿梭机」、「忆梦安眠饮料」等非官方入梦道具。
t_en: Please do not trust any unauthorized dreaming devices such as the "Quantum Shuttle" or the "Sweetdream Drink."

t_ch: 匹诺康尼的梦境结构复杂，不稳定的通道将大概率导致难以预估的后果。
t_en: Since the dream structure within Penacony is complex, unauthorized entries may lead to unpredictable consequences.

t_ch: 每年都有近百位偷渡客被救出，其后续治疗费用并不在旅游保险范围内。
t_en: Each year, we rescue nearly a hundred stowaways, and the costs of their treatment are not covered by insurance.

t_ch: 这玩意不会有危险吧？
t_en: Is this device safe enough?

t_ch: 家族会为您提供最正规、最安全的入梦服务。入梦期间，护理师会负责照看您留在现实中的身体。
t_en: Please feel assured. The Family offers the most secure authorized service, along with a flawless dreaming experience. During your dive, a nurse will attend to your physical body in reality.

t_ch: 如果您在体验梦境时感受到头晕、恶心、呕吐等不适感，请及时脱离梦境，寻求护理师的帮助。
t_en: If you experience any discomfort such as dizziness, nausea, or vomiting, please leave the Dreamscape and seek assistance from your nurse.

t_ch: 对于各类突发状况，酒店都有完备的应对方案，您可尽情享受梦中时光。
t_en: We have prepared a comprehensive emergency plan to handle various situations, ensuring that you can fully enjoy your dream time.

t_ch: 但如果您刚从梦中醒来，请牢记——此处为现实。
t_en: However, upon waking up from a dream, please remember that you are back in reality.

t_ch: 就先到这里吧。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 没问题。入梦须知已记录在您的梦境护照上，您可以随时查阅。
t_en: Certainly. Please refer to your Dreamscape Pass for instructions.

t_ch: 就这样吧，拜拜。
t_en: That's all. Bye-bye.

t_ch: 随时为您服务，尊贵的客人。
t_en: I'm always here to assist you, esteemed guest.

t_ch: 如果您刚从梦中醒来，请牢记——此处是现实。
t_en: If you've just woken up from a dream, remember — this is reality.

t_ch: 为我介绍下「入梦」服务。
t_en: Please allow me to introduce you to our dream services.

t_ch: 根据字面意义，入梦即进入梦境。
t_en: Well, "dream" refers to the process of entering the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 酒店每间客房内都配备特殊「入梦池」，引导客人前往指定梦境。
t_en: Each room is equipped with a specialized "Dreampool" to guide our esteemed guests into the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 家族会为您提供最正规、最安全的服务，以及最完美的梦境体验。
t_en: The Family offers the most secure authorized service, along with a flawless dream experience.

t_ch: 还有非正规入梦吗？
t_en: So, are there "unauthorized" services?

t_ch: 近日有位蓝发「商人」四处兜售「价格低廉，功能可靠」的新式入梦工具。
t_en: Well, recently, a blue-haired "merchant" has been selling a new type of "affordable and reliable" dreaming device.

t_ch: 目前受害者已达十余人，猎犬已经介入调查，但嫌疑人尚未被逮捕归案。
t_en: Over ten individuals have fallen victim to this scam. The Bloodhounds are investigating, but the suspect remains at large.

t_ch: 此外，请勿轻信「量子穿梭机」、「忆梦安眠饮料」等非官方入梦道具。
t_en: Please do not trust any unauthorized dreaming devices such as the "Quantum Shuttle" or the "Sweetdream Drink."

t_ch: 匹诺康尼的梦境结构复杂，不稳定的通道将大概率导致难以预估的后果。
t_en: Since the dream structure within Penacony is complex, unauthorized entries may lead to unpredictable consequences.

t_ch: 每年都有近百位偷渡客被救出，其后续治疗费用并不在旅游保险范围内。
t_en: Each year, we rescue nearly a hundred stowaways, and the costs of their treatment are not covered by insurance.

t_ch: 有什么注意事项吗？
t_en: Is there anything I should keep in mind?

t_ch: 1、请保持心境平和，以免造成非常规的入梦感受。
t_en: 1. Please keep your mind calm to avoid unusual experiences.

t_ch: 2、请保持面部朝上，避免在入梦池中溺水的风险。
t_en: 2. Please ensure that your face is positioned upwards to prevent any risk of drowning in the Dreampool.

t_ch: 3、请勿服用助眠药物，若长时间无法入梦，请联系工作人员。
t_en: 3. Please do not take sleep-aid medication. If you are unable to fall asleep, please contact our staff for assistance.

t_ch: 入梦须知已记录在您的梦境护照上，您可以随时查阅。
t_en: Please refer to your Dreamscape Pass for instructions.

t_ch: 真想一直待在梦里。
t_en: If only I could stay in the dream forever.

t_ch: 据统计，<unbreak>86.9%</unbreak>的宾客持有与您相同的意见。
t_en: According to statistics, <unbreak>86.9%</unbreak> of our guests share the same sentiment as you.

t_ch: 但作为护理师，我建议您至少保持每个系统月内至少7小时的现实活动时间。
t_en: However, as a nurse, I recommend maintaining a minimum of 7 hours of real-world activity time each system month.

t_ch: 过度沉迷梦境大概率会让您疏于管理现实中的身体，甚至引起小脑萎缩、肌肉退化、零件锈蚀等负面反应。
t_en: Excessive immersion in dreams can lead to negative effects such as cerebellar atrophy, muscle degeneration, and overall deterioration.

t_ch: 偶尔有来宾因常年沉浸于梦中，其大脑已变得无法分辨梦境与现实的区别。
t_en: In rare cases, prolonged exposure to dreams can cause guests to struggle in differentiating between dreams and reality.

t_ch: 虽然此类事故的发生率不高于<unbreak>1.5%</unbreak>，但依旧值得警醒。
t_en: Although the occurrence rate is no higher than <unbreak>1.5%</unbreak>, it is still a matter of concern.

t_ch: 所以…如果您刚从梦中醒来，请牢记——此处为现实。
t_en: Therefore... upon waking up from a dream, please remember that you are back in reality.

t_ch: 就先到这里吧。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 好的，随时为您服务。
t_en: Certainly. I'm always here to assist you.

t_ch: 再见。
t_en: Thank you.

t_ch: 女人脸上依旧挂着得体的微笑，但不知为何在你看来显得有些虚弱苍白。
t_en: The woman still wears a polite smile, but you notice a hint of fatigue in her expression.

t_ch: 下次有机会再聊吧。
t_en: Catch you again next time.

t_ch: 再见，祝您在匹诺康尼度过一个愉快的假期。
t_en: See you next time. We wish you a pleasant stay in Penacony.

t_ch: 哎，你看昨晚的机动球比赛了吗？那可是顶级盛宴，不容错过！
t_en: Hey, did you watch the Roboball game last night? It was an outstanding spectacle that must not be missed!

t_ch: 机动球？那是什么…
t_en: Roboball? What is that...?

t_ch: 什么？！竟然真有人不知道机动球！你到底是从哪个没通网的偏远星系来的……
t_en: What!? You don't know what Roboball is? Which peripheral planetary system did you come from...?

t_ch: 「泰科铵机动球」可是风靡全宇宙的竞技项目！
t_en: Taikiyan Roboball is the most popular sport in the entire universe!

t_ch: 这里还有机动球比赛？
t_en: Wait, they even have Roboball matches here?
